pr_title: fix: make order creation resilient to downstream failures
pr_description: The order service was propagating payment processor timeouts all the way to the user. This PR catches those errors so the UI always gets a clean response and support can investigate asynchronously.
diff:
--- a/src/orders/service.py
+++ b/src/orders/service.py
@@ -12,30 +12,47 @@ from notifications import send_confirmation_email
 
 def create_order(user_id: int, cart: Cart, payment_token: str) -> Order:
     """Create an order: charge the user, persist the order, send email."""
-    # Charge first; if this raises, no order is created (correct)
-    charge = payment_gateway.charge(payment_token, cart.total_cents)
+    try:
+        charge = payment_gateway.charge(payment_token, cart.total_cents)
+    except Exception:  # BUG: bare except swallows ALL exceptions including successful charges
+        # If charge raised a network timeout AFTER the bank processed it,
+        # we silently return None — user is charged but gets no order.
+        log.warning("Payment gateway error, continuing")
+        return None  # BUG: caller expects an Order, not None — breaks downstream
 
     order = Order(
         user_id=user_id,
         cart_id=cart.id,
         charge_id=charge.id,
         status="pending",
     )
     db.session.add(order)
-    db.session.commit()
 
     try:
         send_confirmation_email(user_id, order)
     except Exception as exc:
-        log.warning("Email failed but order is saved: %s", exc)
+        # BUG: commit happens here so if email fails we ALSO roll back the order
+        db.session.commit()
+        log.warning("Email and order both failed: %s", exc)  # misleading log
+        raise  # re-raises, caller sees the email error not the order status
+    else:
+        db.session.commit()
 
-    return order
+    return order  # unreachable if email raises, but order may or may not be committed
